rawMetadata: {"compiler":{"version":"0.8.33+commit.64118f21"},"language":"Solidity","output":{"abi":[{"inputs":[],"name":"collect","outputs":[{"internalType":"bytes","name":"","type":"bytes"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes[]","name":"data","type":"bytes[]"}],"name":"shouldRespond","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"","type":"bytes"}],"stateMutability":"pure","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"details":"Detection vectors:   1. Implementation compromise \u2014 Safe-specific functions revert after      masterCopy swap to a contract that doesn't implement them.   2. Subtle masterCopy swap \u2014 Reads slot 0 directly via getStorageAt()      to detect swaps where the new implementation still has Safe functions.   3. Module additions \u2014 Monitors getModulesPaginated() for unauthorized      module installations that could bypass multisig requirements.   4. Guard removal/change \u2014 Reads the guard storage slot to detect      removal or replacement of transaction guards.   5. Threshold/owner manipulation \u2014 Hashes the full owner array and      tracks threshold to detect any signer set changes.   6. Catastrophic balance drain (>5%) \u2014 Single-block drop threshold.   7. Gradual drain (>15%) \u2014 Compares newest vs oldest snapshot across      the full data[] window to catch slow bleeds.   8. Nonce jump \u2014 Detects >5 nonce increment between blocks, indicating      rapid transaction execution (potential batch exploit). Fork reference blocks:   - Block 21,895,237 \u2014 pre-exploit (balances intact, implementation valid)   - Block 21,895,238 \u2014 masterCopy swapped to malicious implementation   - Block 21,895,256 \u2014 ETH drained (18-block window for Drosera response)   - Exploit TX: 0x46deef0f52e3a983b67abf4714448a41dd7ffd6d32d32da69d62081c68ad7882 Key addresses:   - Victim wallet:            0x1Db92e2EeBC8E0c075a02BeA49a2935BcD2dFCF4   - Attacker EOA:             0x0fa09c3a328792253f8dee7116848723b72a6d2e   - Malicious implementation: 0xbDd077f651EBe7f7b3cE16fe5F2b025BE2969516   - Storage manipulator:      0x96221423681A6d52E184D440a8eFCEbB105C7242","kind":"dev","methods":{"collect()":{"details":"This function is expected to be implemented by the contract inheriting this interface."},"shouldRespond(bytes[])":{"details":"This function evaluates the provided data and returns a boolean indicating if a response is needed. The data is arranged where the first element in the array is the most recent data, and the last element is the oldest.","params":{"data":"The abi encoded data to evaluate."}}},"title":"BybitSafeTrap","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"collect()":{"notice":"Collect function to gather data from the trap for a block."},"shouldRespond(bytes[])":{"notice":"Determines if the trap should respond based on the provided data."}},"notice":"Drosera trap that detects Safe{Wallet} proxy compromise across         multiple attack vectors \u2014 modeled on the Bybit $1.46B hack         (February 21, 2025) and generalized for broader multisig protection.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"src/BybitSafeTrap.sol":"BybitSafeTrap"},"evmVersion":"prague","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":false,"runs":200},"remappings":[":drosera-contracts/=node_modules/drosera-contracts/src/",":forge-std/=node_modules/forge-std/src/"]},"sources":{"node_modules/drosera-contracts/src/interfaces/ITrap.sol":{"keccak256":"0xd9b721aa99b335faa62d075696744e25a45e64a666f49bbf64f74b2382ca1b61","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://d1cdfbf39b4e05a0627d17998587adff461ebf7e74b22b72e0cb6839427a46f5","dweb:/ipfs/Qmc8inn3cSbrAyKtXWfwkcor8UVbVkufNBL8iKXY34edq6"]},"src/BybitSafeTrap.sol":{"keccak256":"0x62f215ffbb3632d80626eb98592670d6cb1a1457e80e4e8638919c66458ad47d","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://5cc091dc27936b017fb0e6a6ba34329b35359df7e3c070cb97233899dbdb82b5","dweb:/ipfs/QmWUPGksmNg8APDvkzY3dSFXmcPDxKwo7YjqCK2v3nyBq8"]}},"version":1}

collect().details: This function is expected to be implemented by the contract inheriting this interface.

shouldRespond(bytes[]).details: This function evaluates the provided data and returns a boolean indicating if a response is needed. The data is arranged where the first element in the array is the most recent data, and the last element is the oldest.

params.data: The abi encoded data to evaluate.

collect().notice: Collect function to gather data from the trap for a block.

shouldRespond(bytes[]).notice: Determines if the trap should respond based on the provided data.
